trace.rationale: Historical data shows that in MLB games, the first inning has a run scored approximately 55% of the time, based on general patterns from pre-cutoff seasons. No specific team or pitcher adjustments are available.
Historical MLB data shows roughly 52% of first innings have at least one run scored, with no specific team or pitcher information to adjust this baseline.
Historical MLB data shows roughly 52% of games have a run scored in the first inning, with no strong evidence to deviate for this specific matchup.
Historical MLB data shows roughly 55% of first innings have at least one run scored, with no specific team or pitcher context provided.
Historical MLB data shows roughly 55% of games have a run scored in the first inning, with no specific team or pitcher information to adjust from that baseline.
